# ViewSpec

> ViewSpec is local agent HTML governance first, with an agent-native UI IR and hosted semantic compiler available for advanced agentic engineering workflows.

ViewSpec lets agents emit ordinary HTML, then runs a deterministic local SDK pipeline: `viewspec compile`, `viewspec lift`, `viewspec diff`, and `viewspec check`. The local compiler sanitizes hostile HTML, applies `DESIGN.md`, writes `provenance_manifest.json`, and reports semantic changes without SDK network calls.

The older IntentBundle and CompositionIR model is still the advanced compiler contract for hosted API and cross-platform emitters.

## Canonical Links

- Product and playground: https://viewspec.dev/
- Local HTML governance docs: https://github.com/nxrobins/viewspec/blob/main/docs/local-html-wedge.md
- Cross-platform launch demo: https://viewspec.dev/cross-platform-dashboard/
- Custom motif demo: https://viewspec.dev/custom-motifs/
- Interactive compose demo: https://viewspec.dev/interactive-compose/
- Hosted compiler API: https://api.viewspec.dev/v1/compile
- OpenAPI description: https://viewspec.dev/openapi.json
- Agent system prompt: https://viewspec.dev/agent-system-prompt.txt
- Agent IntentBundle schema: https://viewspec.dev/agent-intent-bundle.schema.json
- Python package: https://pypi.org/project/viewspec/
- Source repository: https://github.com/nxrobins/viewspec
- Full AI context: https://viewspec.dev/llms-full.txt

## Current Product Facts

- Free SDK: `pip install viewspec`
- Runtime LLM calls: zero in local compile/lift/diff/check and zero in the live compiler path.
- Agents should emit HTML for the local governance path, then run `viewspec compile`, `viewspec diff`, and `viewspec check`.
- Native agent setup: `viewspec init-agent --target codex|claude-code|cursor|copilot|all`.
- Optional MCP server: install `viewspec[agents]`, then run `viewspec mcp`.
- Agents should output IntentBundle JSON, not CompositionIR, only when using the advanced hosted/API compiler contract.
- Agents may consult MCP-accessible UI reference libraries (e.g. Lazyweb at https://www.lazyweb.com) for grounded design context before generating advanced IntentBundles. References inform semantic intent only.
- V1 public SDK motifs: table, dashboard, outline, comparison.
- Hosted launch emitters: HTML/Tailwind, React TSX, SwiftUI, Flutter.
- Free hosted tier: 500 calls/day.
- Pro tier: $149/month, hosted compile calls, mobile emitters, and 5 custom motif instances per compile.
- Enterprise tier: custom volume, unlimited custom motifs, org sharing, and launch support.

## Short Description

ViewSpec is local governance for agent-generated HTML: it turns AI-written pages into sanitized, themed, auditable artifacts, with agent-native UI IR and hosted cross-platform compilation available when teams need deeper compiler contracts.
